# RF Satellite Detection Tracks Iranian Dark Vessels in EEZ Despite AIS Blackout

## Overview
A report on April 30, 2026, highlights Iran's use of AIS-dark vessels in the Indian Exclusive Economic Zone. RF collection from satellites approximates vessel locations even when AIS transponders remain fully disabled.[2]

## Details
Shadow fleets, including Iranian vessels, disable AIS transponders and repaint hulls to evade detection, but RF satellite constellations like Spire detect them via electronic emissions from onboard devices.[1][2] RF signals from personal devices, such as those running apps like Candy Crush, enable geolocation through advertising intelligence and signals intelligence integration.[1] This capability remains part of systems like Palantir's Maven Smart System, which fuses RF data with satellite imagery for maritime targeting.[1] The Indian EEZ spans 2.4 million square kilometers, where AIS alone remains insufficient for monitoring dark vessels.[2] Such RF detection supports full AIS darkness scenarios specifically tied to Iran on this date.[2]

## Location
Indian EEZ (2.4 million square kilometers)

## Source Context
Reporting attributed to coverage tracked under @HawkEye360 (HawkEye360). Primary citation provides authoritative public reporting on this development. Cross-source verification through Perplexity sonar-indexed news outlets reinforces the account.
